# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.177.0/http/server.ts";

const knownModules = new Map<string, string>([
  ["moduleA", "https://deno.land/x/udmi@v1.0.0/mod.ts"], // Example public module
  ["myCustomModule", "https://deno.land/x/udmi@v1.0.0/src/example.ts"], // Another example
]);

async function handler(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const searchParams = url.searchParams;

  // 1. Get module paths from environment variables
  const envModulePaths = Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATHS")?.split(",") || [];

  // 2. Get module paths from query parameters
  const queryModulePaths = searchParams.getAll("modulePath");

  // Combine and deduplicate module paths
  const allModulePaths = [...new Set([...envModulePaths, ...queryModulePaths])];

  const loadedModules: { [key: string]: any } = {};
  const errors: { [key: string]: string } = {};

  if (allModulePaths.length === 0) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        message:
          "No module paths provided. Use MODULE_PATHS environment variable or 'modulePath' query parameter.",
        exampleEnv: "MODULE_PATHS=moduleA,https://example.com/someModule.ts",
        exampleQuery: "/?modulePath=moduleA&modulePath=https://example.com/another.ts",
        availableKnownModules: Array.from(knownModules.keys()),
      }),
      {
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        status: 400,
      },
    );
  }

  for (const modulePath of allModulePaths) {
    let finalModuleUrl = modulePath;

    // Resolve "friendly" names to full URLs if they are in knownModules
    if (knownModules.has(modulePath)) {
      finalModuleUrl = knownModules.get(modulePath)!;
    } else if (
      !modulePath.startsWith("http://") && !modulePath.startsWith("https://")
    ) {
      // If it's not a known module and not a full URL, we can't load it.
      errors[modulePath] = "Invalid module path: must be a known module name or a full URL (http/https).";
      continue;
    }

    try {
      console.log(`Attempting to dynamically import: ${finalModuleUrl}`);
      // Dynamic import
      const module = await import(finalModuleUrl);
      loadedModules[modulePath] = module;
      console.log(`Successfully loaded: ${finalModuleUrl}`);
    } catch (e) {
      errors[modulePath] = `Failed to load module: ${e.message || e}`;
      console.error(`Error loading ${finalModuleUrl}:`, e);
    }
  }

  const responseBody = {
    message: "Dynamic module loading attempt completed.",
    requested_modules: allModulePaths,
    loaded_modules_keys: Object.keys(loadedModules),
    successful_loads: Object.fromEntries(
      Object.entries(loadedModules).map(([key, mod]) => [
        key,
        {
          // We can't serialize the entire module object directly,
          // so we extract some information or specific exports.
          // For a real plugin system, you might expect a default export
          // or a specific named export like `pluginInit` or `run`.
          defaultExportExists: "default" in mod,
          namedExports: Object.keys(mod).filter((name) => name !== "default"),
          // Example: if a module has a `run` function, call it and get its output
          runOutput: typeof mod.run === "function" ? mod.run("hello from deploy") : undefined,
          moduleUrl: (mod.meta as ImportMeta | undefined)?.url || finalModuleUrl,
        },
      ]),
    ),
    errors: errors,
  };

  return new Response(JSON.stringify(responseBody, null, 2), {
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    status: Object.keys(errors).length > 0 ? 500 : 200,
  });
}

serve(handler);
